content.text:
First, implement explicit CSRF defenses—such as a synchronizer token or strict Origin/Referer validation—on all state-changing endpoints, and update the authentication flow to explicitly invalidate the old session and generate a new one upon login to eliminate the session fixation risk. While you're in the foundational layers, permanently purge all legacy V1 schema artifacts (services, bookings, attachments) from the database migrations and seed files; they are generating architectural noise and must be completely removed to solidify the strict order-centric design.

With the foundation secured, resolve the remaining concurrency and business logic gaps by refactoring the points balance update logic to use strictly DB-atomic operations (e.g., UPDATE ... SET points_balance = points_balance + ?) to prevent race conditions. Additionally, fix the idempotency cache key mismatch so 404 responses properly utilize the orderId in their scoped key, expand the domain model's role set beyond just photographers to accommodate general service providers, and implement explicit database fields for delivery and pickup ETAs instead of relying on duration-based UI text. Finally, prove these fixes work by writing targeted API and integration tests covering CSRF/session rotation, concurrent points ledger adjustments, SSE stream reconnections, and the 404 idempotency replay path.

38	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;

41	    id                BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,

55	    FOREIGN KEY (photographer_id) REFERENCES users(id),

1	-- Migration V2: Listings, Orders, Messaging, Blacklist, Points, Addresses, Notifications
2	USE booking_portal;
3	
4	-- =====================================================
5	-- LISTINGS: photographer-owned service offerings
6	-- =====================================================
7	CREATE TABLE listings (
8	    id                BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
9	    photographer_id   BIGINT        NOT NULL,
10	    title             VARCHAR(255)  NOT NULL,
11	    description       TEXT,
12	    category          VARCHAR(100),
13	    price             DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
14	    duration_minutes  INT           NOT NULL DEFAULT 60,
15	    location          VARCHAR(500),
16	    max_concurrent    INT           NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
17	    active            BOOLEAN       DEFAULT TRUE,
18	    created_at        TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
19	    updated_at        TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
20	    FOREIGN KEY (photographer_id) REFERENCES users(id)
21	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
22	
23	CREATE INDEX idx_listings_photographer ON listings(photographer_id);
24	CREATE INDEX idx_listings_category ON listings(category);
25	CREATE INDEX idx_listings_active ON listings(active);
26	
27	-- =====================================================
28	-- TIME SLOTS: inventory for oversell prevention
29	-- =====================================================
30	CREATE TABLE time_slots (
31	    id              BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
32	    listing_id      BIGINT    NOT NULL,
33	    slot_date       DATE      NOT NULL,
34	    start_time      TIME      NOT NULL,
35	    end_time        TIME      NOT NULL,
36	    capacity        INT       NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
37	    booked_count    INT       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
38	    version         INT       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
39	    created_at      TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
40	    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
41	    UNIQUE KEY uk_slot (listing_id, slot_date, start_time)
42	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
43	
44	CREATE INDEX idx_timeslots_date ON time_slots(slot_date);
45	
46	-- =====================================================
47	-- ORDERS: strict FSM lifecycle
48	-- =====================================================
49	CREATE TABLE orders (
50	    id                  BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
51	    order_number        VARCHAR(50)   NOT NULL UNIQUE,
52	    customer_id         BIGINT        NOT NULL,
53	    photographer_id     BIGINT        NOT NULL,
54	    listing_id          BIGINT        NOT NULL,
55	    time_slot_id        BIGINT        NOT NULL,
56	    status              VARCHAR(30)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 'CREATED',
57	    total_price         DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
58	    paid_amount         DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0.00,
59	    payment_reference   VARCHAR(255),
60	    cancel_reason       TEXT,
61	    refund_amount       DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0.00,
62	    reschedule_from_id  BIGINT,
63	    address_id          BIGINT,
64	    notes               TEXT,
65	    payment_deadline    TIMESTAMP     NULL,
66	    created_at          TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
67	    updated_at          TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
68	    FOREIGN KEY (customer_id)     REFERENCES users(id),
69	    FOREIGN KEY (photographer_id) REFERENCES users(id),
70	    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id)      REFERENCES listings(id),
71	    FOREIGN KEY (time_slot_id)    REFERENCES time_slots(id)
72	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
73	
74	CREATE INDEX idx_orders_customer ON orders(customer_id);
75	CREATE INDEX idx_orders_photographer ON orders(photographer_id);
76	CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status);
77	CREATE INDEX idx_orders_payment_deadline ON orders(payment_deadline);
78	
79	-- =====================================================
80	-- ORDER ACTIONS: audit trail for every state change
81	-- =====================================================
82	CREATE TABLE order_actions (
83	    id              BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
84	    order_id        BIGINT       NOT NULL,
85	    action          VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
86	    from_status     VARCHAR(30),
87	    to_status       VARCHAR(30)  NOT NULL,
88	    performed_by    BIGINT       NOT NULL,
89	    detail          TEXT,
90	    created_at      TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
91	    FOREIGN KEY (order_id)     REFERENCES orders(id),
92	    FOREIGN KEY (performed_by) REFERENCES users(id)
93	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
94	
95	CREATE INDEX idx_order_actions_order ON order_actions(order_id);
96	
97	-- =====================================================
98	-- IDEMPOTENCY TOKENS: 10-minute validity per action
99	-- =====================================================
100	CREATE TABLE idempotency_tokens (
101	    token           VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,
102	    order_id        BIGINT,
103	    action          VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
104	    response_status INT,
105	    response_body   TEXT,
106	    created_at      TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
107	    expires_at      TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL
108	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
109	
110	CREATE INDEX idx_idemp_expires ON idempotency_tokens(expires_at);
111	
112	-- =====================================================
113	-- ADDRESSES
114	-- =====================================================
115	CREATE TABLE addresses (
116	    id            BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
117	    user_id       BIGINT       NOT NULL,
118	    label         VARCHAR(100),
119	    street        VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
120	    city          VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
121	    state         VARCHAR(100),
122	    postal_code   VARCHAR(20),
123	    country       VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'US',
124	    is_default    BOOLEAN      DEFAULT FALSE,
125	    created_at    TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
126	    updated_at    TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
127	    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
128	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
129	
130	CREATE INDEX idx_addresses_user ON addresses(user_id);
131	
132	-- =====================================================
133	-- CONVERSATIONS & MESSAGES
134	-- =====================================================
135	CREATE TABLE conversations (
136	    id                BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
137	    participant_one   BIGINT    NOT NULL,
138	    participant_two   BIGINT    NOT NULL,
139	    order_id          BIGINT,
140	    last_message_at   TIMESTAMP NULL,
141	    created_at        TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
142	    FOREIGN KEY (participant_one) REFERENCES users(id),
143	    FOREIGN KEY (participant_two) REFERENCES users(id),
144	    FOREIGN KEY (order_id)        REFERENCES orders(id),
145	    UNIQUE KEY uk_conversation (participant_one, participant_two, order_id)
146	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
147	
148	CREATE INDEX idx_conv_p1 ON conversations(participant_one);
149	CREATE INDEX idx_conv_p2 ON conversations(participant_two);
150	
151	CREATE TABLE messages (
152	    id                BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
153	    conversation_id   BIGINT       NOT NULL,
154	    sender_id         BIGINT       NOT NULL,
155	    content           TEXT         NOT NULL,
156	    read_at           TIMESTAMP    NULL,
157	    created_at        TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
158	    FOREIGN KEY (conversation_id) REFERENCES conversations(id),
159	    FOREIGN KEY (sender_id)       REFERENCES users(id)
160	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
161	
162	CREATE INDEX idx_messages_conv ON messages(conversation_id);
163	CREATE INDEX idx_messages_unread ON messages(conversation_id, read_at);
164	
165	-- =====================================================
166	-- NOTIFICATION QUEUE: local queued to-send records
167	-- =====================================================
168	CREATE TABLE notification_queue (
169	    id              BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
170	    user_id         BIGINT        NOT NULL,
171	    channel         VARCHAR(10)   NOT NULL COMMENT 'EMAIL or SMS',
172	    recipient       VARCHAR(255)  NOT NULL COMMENT 'email address or phone number (encrypted)',
173	    subject         VARCHAR(500),
174	    body            TEXT          NOT NULL,
175	    status          VARCHAR(20)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 'QUEUED' COMMENT 'QUEUED, SENT, FAILED',
176	    retry_count     INT           DEFAULT 0,
177	    reference_type  VARCHAR(50),
178	    reference_id    BIGINT,
179	    created_at      TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
180	    processed_at    TIMESTAMP     NULL

1	-- Migration V6: Server-side search terms store and address uniqueness constraint
2	USE booking_portal;
3	
4	-- =====================================================
5	-- SEARCH TERMS: global popular terms ranked by frequency/recency
6	-- =====================================================
7	CREATE TABLE search_terms (
8	    id            BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
9	    term          VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
10	    frequency     INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
11	    last_used_at  TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
12	    created_at    TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
13	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
14	
15	CREATE INDEX idx_search_terms_popular ON search_terms(frequency DESC, last_used_at DESC);
16	
17	-- =====================================================
18	-- ADDRESS DEFAULT UNIQUENESS: only one default per user
19	-- =====================================================
20	-- Strategy: use a BEFORE INSERT and BEFORE UPDATE trigger to enforce
21	-- at most one is_default=true per user_id. MySQL unique indexes treat
22	-- NULL as distinct, so a generated-column approach cannot reliably
23	-- prevent multiple defaults. Triggers provide a deterministic guarantee.
24	
25	DELIMITER //
26	
27	CREATE TRIGGER trg_address_default_insert
28	BEFORE INSERT ON addresses
29	FOR EACH ROW
30	BEGIN
31	    IF NEW.is_default = TRUE THEN
32	        IF (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM addresses
33	            WHERE user_id = NEW.user_id AND is_default = TRUE) > 0 THEN
34	            SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000'
35	            SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Another default address already exists for this user';
36	        END IF;
37	    END IF;
38	END//
39	
40	CREATE TRIGGER trg_address_default_update
41	BEFORE UPDATE ON addresses
42	FOR EACH ROW
43	BEGIN
44	    IF NEW.is_default = TRUE AND (OLD.is_default = FALSE OR OLD.is_default IS NULL) THEN
45	        IF (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM addresses
46	            WHERE user_id = NEW.user_id AND is_default = TRUE AND id != NEW.id) > 0 THEN
47	            SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000'
48	            SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Another default address already exists for this user';
49	        END IF;
50	    END IF;
51	END//
52	
53	DELIMITER ;
54	
55	INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description)
56	VALUES (6, 'Search terms store, address default uniqueness triggers');

1	# Booking Portal
2	
3	Offline-first photography booking platform. Java 17 / Spring MVC / MyBatis / MySQL / jQuery.
4	
5	## One-Command Startup
6	
7	```bash
8	docker compose up
9	```
10	
11	The stack is fully self-contained. No `.env` files, no host-installed JDK, no external APIs. All credentials and encryption keys are generated once by a short-lived init container (`init-secrets`) and shared with both the database and webapp via a tmpfs volume — nothing is persisted to the host filesystem and no environment variable carries an empty fallback.
12	
13	| Service | Container | Lifecycle | Purpose |
14	|---------|-----------|-----------|---------|
15	| **Init Secrets** | `booking-init-secrets` | Runs once, then exits | Generates all credentials to `/run/secrets/booking/secrets.env` |
16	| **MySQL 8.0** | `booking-mysql` | Long-running | Persistent data store (port **3306**) |
17	| **Web Application** | `booking-webapp` | Long-running | Spring Boot app, jQuery SPA (port **8080**) |
18	
19	Open **http://localhost:8080** after startup.
20	
21	## Initialization Behavior
22	
23	On first `docker compose up`:
24	
25	1. **`init-secrets` container** (`docker/init-secrets.sh`) generates all runtime secrets via `/dev/urandom` and writes them to `/run/secrets/booking/secrets.env` on a shared tmpfs volume:
26	   - `MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD` — 24 random bytes, hex-encoded (48 chars)
27	   - `MYSQL_USER` — `booking_app` (static)
28	   - `MYSQL_PASSWORD` — 24 random bytes, hex-encoded (48 chars)
29	   - `ENCRYPTION_KEY` — 32 random bytes, hex-encoded (64 chars, exceeds the 32-character minimum)
30	   - If the secrets file already exists (restart without `down -v`), generation is skipped and existing credentials are reused.
31	2. **`mysql` container** starts via `docker/mysql-entrypoint.sh`, which sources the shared secrets file into its environment and delegates to MySQL's official `docker-entrypoint.sh`. If the secrets file is missing the container refuses to start. Init scripts run in order:
32	   - `01-schema.sql` through `08-migration-v6.sql`
33	3. MySQL health check (`mysqladmin ping`) must pass before webapp starts.
34	4. **`webapp` container** starts via `docker/entrypoint.sh`, which sources the same shared secrets file. The entrypoint performs pre-flight validation before the JVM launches:
35	   - Refuses to start if `ENCRYPTION_KEY` is shorter than 32 characters.
36	   - Refuses to start if `MYSQL_PASSWORD` is empty.
37	   - Exports `SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME` and `SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD` from the shared credentials.
38	5. Spring context initializes. `SecretsValidator` (`@PostConstruct`) performs a second validation layer inside the JVM:
39	   - Rejects empty `SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME` or `SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD`.
40	   - Rejects `ENCRYPTION_KEY` shorter than 32 characters.
41	   - The application context fails to initialize if any check fails.
42	6. `EncryptionConfig` calls `FieldEncryptor.configure(ENCRYPTION_KEY)`, which derives the 256-bit AES key via PBKDF2. This is a third validation layer — `FieldEncryptor` independently rejects keys shorter than 32 characters.
43	7. `DataInitializer` replaces all `$PLACEHOLDER$` password hashes with real BCrypt hashes for `password123`.
44	8. Scheduled tasks begin: auto-close unpaid orders (2 min), auto-lift expired blacklists (5 min), notification retry (3 min), clean idempotency tokens (15 min).
45	9. Webapp health check (`/actuator/health`) confirms MySQL connectivity.
46	
47	**No environment variable in `docker-compose.yml` carries an empty-string fallback.** The `mysql` and `webapp` services contain no inline credentials — both read from the same `secrets.env` file generated by the init container.
48	
49	To reset all data (including regenerating secrets): `docker compose down -v && docker compose up`
50	
51	## Default Test Users
52	
53	All passwords are **`password123`**.
54	
55	> **WARNING: These are demo credentials for local development only. Never deploy to production with these users or passwords. In production, rotate all secrets, disable seed accounts, and set `ENCRYPTION_KEY` to a cryptographically random value of at least 32 characters. The application enforces this minimum at startup and will fail to boot with a shorter key.**
56	
57	| Username | Role | ID | Purpose |
58	|----------|------|----|---------|
59	| `admin` | ADMINISTRATOR | 1 | Full system access, blacklist, points rules |
60	| `jphoto` | PHOTOGRAPHER | 2 | Owns listings 1, 2, 5; manages own orders |
61	| `sphoto` | PHOTOGRAPHER | 3 | Owns listings 3, 4; separate from jphoto's data |
62	| `customer1` | CUSTOMER | 4 | Has addresses, creates orders |
63	| `customer2` | CUSTOMER | 5 | Second customer for access-control testing |
64	
65	## Running Tests
66	
67	```bash
68	./run_tests.sh          # Full suite (unit + API), 90% coverage gate
69	./run_tests.sh unit     # Unit tests only
70	./run_tests.sh api      # Both suites (API requires unit phase)
71	```
72	
73	Only requires **Docker**. Tests run inside a Maven container with an H2 in-memory database — no MySQL needed.
74	
75	### Test Layout
76	
77	```
78	unit_tests/             # Unit tests (Mockito, no Spring context)
79	  java/com/booking/unit/   28 test classes
80	  resources/               H2 schema + seed data
81	
82	api_tests/              # Integration tests (full Spring Boot + H2)
83	  java/com/booking/api/    14 test classes (including base class)
84	  resources/               application-test.yml, schema, seed data
85	```
86	
87	Tests **must** live in `unit_tests/` or `api_tests/`. The Maven enforcer plugin fails the build if any test file appears under `src/test/java`.
88	
89	### Coverage Gate
90	
91	JaCoCo enforces **>= 90% line coverage** on the merged (unit + API) execution data with no exclusions — all legacy classes have been physically deleted. Coverage reports are written to `test-results/` after each run.
92

94

106	  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
107	  -d '{"username":"customer1","password":"password123"}'

**V6 - Search Terms & Address Uniqueness:** `/docker/mysql/08-migration-v6.sql` (lines 1-57)

**Seed Data (V2):** `/docker/mysql/04-seed-v2.sql`

**Legacy (V1) Artifacts Still in Schema:**

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive view of the entire repository. Let me compile the complete report:

## Comprehensive Repository Exploration Report

I've conducted a thorough exploration of the Booking Portal repository. Here's the detailed breakdown:

### 1. OVERALL PROJECT STRUCTURE

**Framework & Language:**
- Java 17, Spring Boot 3.2.5
- MyBatis 3.0.3 for ORM
- MySQL 8.0 database
- JUnit 5 + Mockito for testing
- jQuery frontend (static assets)

**Key Directories:**
- `/src/main/java/com/booking/` - Main application code (81 Java files)
- `/src/main/resources/mapper/` - MyBatis XML mappers (18 files)
- `/unit_tests/java/com/booking/unit/` - Unit tests (28 test classes)
- `/api_tests/java/com/booking/api/` - Integration tests (14 test classes)
- `/docker/mysql/` - Database migrations (8 SQL files: 01-schema.sql through 08-migration-v6.sql)

**Package Structure:**
- `controller/` - REST endpoints (9 controllers)
- `service/` - Business logic (15 services)
- `mapper/` - MyBatis interfaces (18 mappers)
- `domain/` - Entity models (17 domain classes)
- `util/` - Utilities (RoleGuard, SessionUtil, FieldEncryptor, MaskUtil)
- `filter/` - AuthFilter for request authentication
- `config/` - Spring configuration

---

### 2. AUTHENTICATION & SESSION HANDLING

**Session Management:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/util/SessionUtil.java`
  - `setCurrentUser(HttpSession, User)` - stores user in session attribute "currentUser"
  - `getCurrentUser(HttpSession)` - retrieves authenticated user
  - `isAdmin(session)` - role check helper
  - `hasRole(session, roleName)` - generic role checker

**Authentication Filter:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/filter/AuthFilter.java` (lines 1-76)
  - Allows `/api/auth/*` endpoints without session
  - On every request, revalidates user `enabled` status from DB (line 55)
  - Checks blacklist status (line 65) and expires blacklist entries (line 66)
  - Returns 401 for missing session, 403 for disabled/blacklisted accounts

**Auth Endpoints:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/AuthController.java`
  - `POST /api/auth/login` - authenticates via username/password
  - `POST /api/auth/register` - creates new CUSTOMER account
  - `POST /api/auth/logout` - invalidates session
  - `GET /api/auth/me` - returns current authenticated user

**Role Guard Utilities:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/util/RoleGuard.java`
  - `requireLogin(session)` - throws SecurityException if not authenticated
  - `requireAdmin(session)` - enforces ADMINISTRATOR role
  - `requireRole(session, ...roles)` - enforces one of multiple roles
  - `requireOwnerOrAdmin(session, ownerId)` - owner or admin check

---

### 3. STATE-CHANGING API ENDPOINTS

**Orders (Primary workflow):**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/OrderController.java`
  - `POST /api/orders` - create order (idempotency key required, line 61)
  - `POST /api/orders/{id}/confirm` - photographer confirms (line 96)
  - `POST /api/orders/{id}/pay` - customer records payment (line 103)
  - `POST /api/orders/{id}/check-in` - photographer check-in (line 114)
  - `POST /api/orders/{id}/check-out` - photographer check-out (line 121)
  - `POST /api/orders/{id}/complete` - photographer completes (line 128)
  - `POST /api/orders/{id}/cancel` - customer/photographer/admin cancel (line 135)
  - `POST /api/orders/{id}/refund` - admin refund (line 143)
  - `POST /api/orders/{id}/reschedule` - customer reschedule to new time slot (line 154)

**Points/Awards:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/PointsController.java`
  - `POST /api/points/adjust` - admin adjust balance with mandatory reason (line 74)
  - `POST /api/points/award` - admin award (legacy compat, line 123)

**Listings:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/ListingController.java`
  - `POST /api/listings` - photographer creates listing (line 80)
  - `PUT /api/listings/{id}` - photographer/admin updates listing (line 92)

**Time Slots:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/TimeSlotController.java`
  - `POST /api/timeslots` - create time slot (photography owner or admin, line 38)

**Messages & Chat:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/MessageController.java`
  - `POST /api/messages/send` - send direct message to user (line 90)
  - `POST /api/messages/conversations/{id}/reply` - reply in conversation (line 104)
  - `POST /api/messages/conversations/{id}/image` - upload chat image attachment (line 118)

**Notifications:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/NotificationController.java`
  - `POST /api/notifications/{id}/read` - mark notification as read (line 36)
  - `POST /api/notifications/{id}/archive` - archive notification (line 43)
  - `PUT /api/notifications/preferences` - update notification preferences (line 68)
  - `POST /api/notifications/export` - admin marks batch as exported (line 91)

**Addresses:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/AddressController.java`
  - `POST /api/addresses` - create address (line 41)
  - `PUT /api/addresses/{id}` - update address (line 51)
  - `DELETE /api/addresses/{id}` - delete address (line 65)

**Blacklist (Admin):**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/BlacklistController.java`
  - `POST /api/blacklist` - admin blacklist user (line 45)
  - `POST /api/blacklist/{id}/lift` - admin lift blacklist (line 62)

**Users (Admin):**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/UserController.java`
  - `PATCH /api/users/{id}` - admin patch update user (line 52)
  - `PUT /api/users/{id}` - admin full update (line 66)
  - `PATCH /api/users/{id}/enabled` - admin enable/disable account (line 80)

---

### 4. DATABASE MIGRATIONS & SCHEMA ARTIFACTS

**Schema Versions (8 migrations):**

**V1 - Initial Schema:** `/docker/mysql/01-schema.sql` (lines 1-86)
- `roles` table (id, name, description)
- `users` table (with role_id FK)
- **Legacy artifacts:** `services` table (line 30), `bookings` table (line 40-62), `attachments` table (line 64-76)
- `schema_version` tracking table

**V2 - Orders & Listings Model:** `/docker/mysql/03-migration-v2.sql` (lines 1-230)
- `listings` table (photographer_id, title, price, duration, location, active)
- `time_slots` table (listing_id FK, slot_date, capacity, booked_count, version for optimistic locking)
- `orders` table - **NEW FSM-based model** (customer_id, photographer_id, listing_id, time_slot_id, status, payment_deadline, delivery_mode)
- `order_actions` table (audit trail, performed_by FK)
- `idempotency_tokens` table (token PK, action, response_status, response_body, expires_at)
- `addresses` table (user_id, label, street, city, state, postal_code, is_default)
- `conversations` table (participant_one, participant_two, order_id, last_message_at)
- `messages` table (conversation_id FK, sender_id FK, content, read_at)
- `notification_queue` table (user_id, channel EMAIL/SMS, recipient, status QUEUED/SENT/FAILED, retry_count)
- `blacklist` table (user_id, reason, blacklisted_by, duration_days, starts_at, expires_at, active, lifted_by, lifted_at)
- `points_ledger` table (user_id, points, balance_after, action, reference_type, reference_id)
- ALTER users: ADD `points_balance`

**V3 - Notification Preferences & Points Rules:** `/docker/mysql/05-migration-v3.sql` (lines 1-83)
- `notification_preferences` table (user_id UNIQUE, order_updates, holds, reminders, approvals, compliance=TRUE, mute_non_critical)
- `chat_attachments` table (message_id FK, file_name, original_name, content_type, file_size)
- `points_rules` table (name UNIQUE, scope INDIVIDUAL/CLASS/DEPARTMENT/TEAM, trigger_event, points, active)
- `points_adjustments` table (user_id, adjusted_by, points, reason mandatory, balance_before, balance_after)
- ALTER users: ADD `department`, `team`
- Default rules seeded: ORDER_PAYMENT (10pts), ORDER_COMPLETED (20pts), FIRST_ORDER (50pts), REFERRAL_BONUS (30pts), TEAM_MILESTONE (100pts), DEPT_EXCELLENCE (200pts)

**V4 - Delivery Modes & Listing Filters:** `/docker/mysql/06-migration-v4.sql` (lines 1-22)
- ALTER orders: ADD `delivery_mode` (ONSITE/PICKUP/COURIER)
- ALTER notification_queue: ADD read_at, archived, terminal (for tracking state)
- ALTER listings: ADD theme, transport_mode, rating, rating_count

**V5 - Structured Location Hierarchy:** `/docker/mysql/07-migration-v5.sql` (lines 1-8)
- ALTER listings: ADD location_state, location_city, location_neighborhood

**V6 - Search Terms & Address Uniqueness:** `/docker/mysql/08-migration-v6.sql` (lines 1-57)
- `search_terms` table (term UNIQUE, frequency, last_used_at, created_at)
- **Triggers for address default uniqueness enforcement** (lines 25-52):
  - `trg_address_default_insert` - prevents multiple default addresses per user on INSERT
  - `trg_address_default_update` - prevents multiple default addresses per user on UPDATE

**Seed Data (V2):** `/docker/mysql/04-seed-v2.sql`
- Sample listings from photographers jphoto (id=2) and sphoto (id=3)
- Sample time slots for availability
- Sample addresses for customers

**Legacy (V1) Artifacts Still in Schema:**
- `services` table (used in old /api/bookings endpoints, now removed)
- `bookings` table (superseded by orders)
- `attachments` table (superseded by chat_attachments)

---

### 5. POINTS BALANCE & LEDGER LOGIC

**Balance Storage & Calculation:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/service/PointsService.java`
  - `getBalance(userId)` - reads from users.points_balance (line 29)
  - `getHistory(userId)` - all ledger entries for user (line 33)
  - `getLeaderboard()` - ranked by points DESC, completion count DESC, earliest completion ASC (line 37)

**Points Award Triggers:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/service/PointsService.java` (lines 46-96)
  - `awardByTrigger(triggerEvent, userId, refType, refId, context)` - scoped by rule.scope:
    - INDIVIDUAL: awards to single user (line 52)
    - DEPARTMENT: awards to all users in same department (line 54-61)
    - TEAM: awards to all users in same team (line 67-78)
    - CLASS: awards to all users with same roleId (line 80-93)

**Ledger Entry Creation:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/service/PointsService.java` (lines 100-137)
  - `awardPoints(userId, points, action, refType, refId, description)` - creates entry, updates balance (line 100)
  - `deductPoints(userId, points, action, refType, refId, description)` - creates negative entry, floor at 0 (line 120)

**Order Integration:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/service/OrderService.java` (line 171, 201, 268-272)
  - On payment recorded: `awardByRule("ORDER_PAID", order)` (line 171)
  - On completion: `awardByRule("ORDER_COMPLETED", order)` (line 201)
  - On refund: deduct ORDER_PAID points from customer (lines 268-272)

**Admin Adjustment:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/PointsController.java` (lines 74-106)
  - `POST /api/points/adjust` - mandatory reason field (line 78)
  - Records `PointsAdjustment` with balance_before, balance_after for audit (line 95-102)

**Mapper:**
- File: `/src/main/resources/mapper/PointsLedgerMapper.xml`
  - `findByUserId` - ordered by created_at DESC (line 6)
  - `getBalance` - reads users.points_balance (line 10)
  - `updateUserBalance` - atomic update (line 19)
  - `getLeaderboard` - complex join with completion count and earliest completion date (lines 23-32)

---

### 6. IDEMPOTENCY HANDLING

**Idempotency Token Model:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/domain/IdempotencyToken.java`
  - token (PK, VARCHAR 100)
  - orderId (FK, nullable for create operations)
  - action (VARCHAR 50)
  - responseStatus, responseBody (cached response)
  - expiresAt (10-minute validity)
  - `isExpired()` - checks against LocalDateTime.now()

**Idempotency Service:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/service/IdempotencyService.java` (lines 1-101)
  - **SCOPED CACHE KEYS:** `checkToken(token, action, orderId)` builds compound key: `token::action::orderId` (or "new" for creation, line 43)
  - **10-minute validity:** TOKEN_VALIDITY_MINUTES = 10 (line 13)
  - `checkToken()` logic (lines 39-68):
    - Returns null cache if expired (line 47)
    - Returns cached response if complete (line 50)
    - Returns 409 if request already in-flight (line 53)
    - Inserts new token on first call, catches duplicate insert exception (line 62-64)
  - `recordResponse(token, action, orderId, status, body)` - updates response on completion (line 76)
  - `cleanupExpired()` - deletes expired tokens (line 98)

**Controller Integration:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/OrderController.java`
  - CREATE order (line 64): `checkToken(idempotencyKey, "CREATE_ORDER", null)` - orderId=null for new
  - Action endpoints (line 171): `checkToken(idempotencyKey, action, orderId)` - includes order ID
  - On 404 (line 180): still records response with status 404, orderId=null

**Mapper:**
- File: `/src/main/resources/mapper/IdempotencyTokenMapper.xml`
  - `findByToken(token)` - compound scoped key lookup
  - `insert(newToken)` - unique constraint on token column
  - `updateResponse(token, status, body)` - atomic update
  - `deleteExpired(now)` - cleanup task

---

### 7. ROLE & USER MODEL

**User Domain:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/domain/User.java`
  - id, username (UNIQUE), email (UNIQUE), passwordHash, fullName, phone
  - roleId (FK to roles)
  - enabled (Boolean, checked on every request by AuthFilter)
  - pointsBalance (Int, default 0)
  - department (VARCHAR 100, nullable - for DEPARTMENT scope points)
  - team (VARCHAR 100, nullable - for TEAM scope points)
  - roleName (joined field from roles.name)
  - **Phone masking:** `getPhoneMasked()` (line 45) attempts decrypt then masks, falls back to plaintext mask (line 50-54)

**Roles:**
- CUSTOMER (id=1) - can create orders, manage own addresses
- PHOTOGRAPHER (id=2) - owns listings, confirms/completes orders, uploads chat images
- ADMINISTRATOR (id=3) - full access, blacklist, points rules

**PhotographerDto (Read-Only Discovery):**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/domain/PhotographerDto.java`
  - Constructor takes User, extracts only: id, username, fullName
  - Shields: email, phone, enabled, passwordHash, account metadata
  - Used for `/api/users/photographers` endpoint (line 34)

**User Service:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/service/UserService.java`
  - `getPhotographers()` - filters users with role_id=2 (PHOTOGRAPHER)
  - `patchUpdate(id, fields)` - admin field updates
  - `setEnabled(id, enabled)` - admin enable/disable

---

### 8. DELIVERY/PICKUP ETA LOGIC

**Delivery Mode in Order:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/domain/Order.java` (line 23)
  - deliveryMode field (VARCHAR 20)

**Order Creation Validation:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/service/OrderService.java` (lines 75-94)
  - VALID_DELIVERY_MODES = {ONSITE, PICKUP, COURIER} (line 21)
  - Defaults to "ONSITE" if not provided (line 78)
  - Enforces address_id required for COURIER mode (line 82)
  - Verifies address ownership (line 91)

**Migration (V4):**
- File: `/docker/mysql/06-migration-v4.sql` (line 5)
  - `ALTER TABLE orders ADD COLUMN delivery_mode VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ONSITE'`

**NOTE: NO ETA LOGIC** - there are no ETA fields or pickup/delivery time calculation functions in the codebase. Delivery mode is tracked but no ETA computation exists.

---

### 9. SSE STREAM HANDLING

**Chat SSE Controller:**
- File: `/src/main/java/com/booking/controller/ChatSseController.java`
  - `GET /api/messages/stream` (line 28)
  - Produces `MediaType.TEXT_EVENT_STREAM_VALUE` (line 28)
  - 5-minute timeout: `new SseEmitter(300_000L)` (line 31)
  - Per-user emitter map: `Map<Long, List<SseEmitter>>` (line 26)
  - Stores emitters per user ID with CopyOnWriteArrayList for thread-safety (line 33)
  - Cleanup callbacks on completion/timeout/error (lines 35-44)
  - Initial keepalive event: `"connected" -> "ok"` (line 48)
  - `pushMessageEvent(userId, payload)` - sends to all active SSE connections for user (line 59)

**Integration with MessageService:**
- Called by MessageService when new message created to push to both participants
- No persistence of SSE connection state

---

### 10. EXISTING TEST STRUCTURE

**Test Organization:**
- File: `/pom.xml` (lines 41-128)
  - Unit tests source: `unit_tests/java` (line 42)
  - API tests source: `api_tests/java` (line 66)
  - Test resources: `unit_tests/resources` and `api_tests/resources` (lines 44-45)

  - Maven Failsafe (lines 111-128): runs `**/api/**/*IT.java`
  - JaCoCo (lines 130-204): enforces 90% line coverage on merged unit+API data (line 197)

  - Test files:
    - `OrderWorkflowApiIT` - order lifecycle, idempotency, oversell (line 18)

    - `SecurityHardeningApiIT` - blacklist checks, IDOR fixes

1	-- Migration V2: Listings, Orders, Messaging, Blacklist, Points, Addresses, Notifications
2	USE booking_portal;
3	
4	-- =====================================================
5	-- LISTINGS: photographer-owned service offerings
6	-- =====================================================
7	CREATE TABLE listings (
8	    id                BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
9	    photographer_id   BIGINT        NOT NULL,
10	    title             VARCHAR(255)  NOT NULL,
11	    description       TEXT,
12	    category          VARCHAR(100),
13	    price             DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
14	    duration_minutes  INT           NOT NULL DEFAULT 60,
15	    location          VARCHAR(500),
16	    max_concurrent    INT           NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
17	    active            BOOLEAN       DEFAULT TRUE,
18	    created_at        TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
19	    updated_at        TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
20	    FOREIGN KEY (photographer_id) REFERENCES users(id)
21	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
22	
23	CREATE INDEX idx_listings_photographer ON listings(photographer_id);
24	CREATE INDEX idx_listings_category ON listings(category);
25	CREATE INDEX idx_listings_active ON listings(active);
26	
27	-- =====================================================
28	-- TIME SLOTS: inventory for oversell prevention
29	-- =====================================================
30	CREATE TABLE time_slots (
31	    id              BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
32	    listing_id      BIGINT    NOT NULL,
33	    slot_date       DATE      NOT NULL,
34	    start_time      TIME      NOT NULL,
35	    end_time        TIME      NOT NULL,
36	    capacity        INT       NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
37	    booked_count    INT       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
38	    version         INT       NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
39	    created_at      TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
40	    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id) REFERENCES listings(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
41	    UNIQUE KEY uk_slot (listing_id, slot_date, start_time)
42	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
43	
44	CREATE INDEX idx_timeslots_date ON time_slots(slot_date);
45	
46	-- =====================================================
47	-- ORDERS: strict FSM lifecycle
48	-- =====================================================
49	CREATE TABLE orders (
50	    id                  BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
51	    order_number        VARCHAR(50)   NOT NULL UNIQUE,
52	    customer_id         BIGINT        NOT NULL,
53	    photographer_id     BIGINT        NOT NULL,
54	    listing_id          BIGINT        NOT NULL,
55	    time_slot_id        BIGINT        NOT NULL,
56	    status              VARCHAR(30)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 'CREATED',
57	    total_price         DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
58	    paid_amount         DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0.00,
59	    payment_reference   VARCHAR(255),
60	    cancel_reason       TEXT,
61	    refund_amount       DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0.00,
62	    reschedule_from_id  BIGINT,
63	    address_id          BIGINT,
64	    notes               TEXT,
65	    payment_deadline    TIMESTAMP     NULL,
66	    created_at          TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
67	    updated_at          TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
68	    FOREIGN KEY (customer_id)     REFERENCES users(id),
69	    FOREIGN KEY (photographer_id) REFERENCES users(id),
70	    FOREIGN KEY (listing_id)      REFERENCES listings(id),
71	    FOREIGN KEY (time_slot_id)    REFERENCES time_slots(id)
72	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
73	
74	CREATE INDEX idx_orders_customer ON orders(customer_id);
75	CREATE INDEX idx_orders_photographer ON orders(photographer_id);
76	CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status);
77	CREATE INDEX idx_orders_payment_deadline ON orders(payment_deadline);
78	
79	-- =====================================================
80	-- ORDER ACTIONS: audit trail for every state change
81	-- =====================================================
82	CREATE TABLE order_actions (
83	    id              BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
84	    order_id        BIGINT       NOT NULL,
85	    action          VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
86	    from_status     VARCHAR(30),
87	    to_status       VARCHAR(30)  NOT NULL,
88	    performed_by    BIGINT       NOT NULL,
89	    detail          TEXT,
90	    created_at      TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
91	    FOREIGN KEY (order_id)     REFERENCES orders(id),
92	    FOREIGN KEY (performed_by) REFERENCES users(id)
93	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
94	
95	CREATE INDEX idx_order_actions_order ON order_actions(order_id);
96	
97	-- =====================================================
98	-- IDEMPOTENCY TOKENS: 10-minute validity per action
99	-- =====================================================
100	CREATE TABLE idempotency_tokens (
101	    token           VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY,
102	    order_id        BIGINT,
103	    action          VARCHAR(50)  NOT NULL,
104	    response_status INT,
105	    response_body   TEXT,
106	    created_at      TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
107	    expires_at      TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL
108	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
109	
110	CREATE INDEX idx_idemp_expires ON idempotency_tokens(expires_at);
111	
112	-- =====================================================
113	-- ADDRESSES
114	-- =====================================================
115	CREATE TABLE addresses (
116	    id            BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
117	    user_id       BIGINT       NOT NULL,
118	    label         VARCHAR(100),
119	    street        VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
120	    city          VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
121	    state         VARCHAR(100),
122	    postal_code   VARCHAR(20),
123	    country       VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'US',
124	    is_default    BOOLEAN      DEFAULT FALSE,
125	    created_at    TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
126	    updated_at    TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
127	    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
128	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
129	
130	CREATE INDEX idx_addresses_user ON addresses(user_id);
131	
132	-- =====================================================
133	-- CONVERSATIONS & MESSAGES
134	-- =====================================================
135	CREATE TABLE conversations (
136	    id                BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
137	    participant_one   BIGINT    NOT NULL,
138	    participant_two   BIGINT    NOT NULL,
139	    order_id          BIGINT,
140	    last_message_at   TIMESTAMP NULL,
141	    created_at        TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
142	    FOREIGN KEY (participant_one) REFERENCES users(id),
143	    FOREIGN KEY (participant_two) REFERENCES users(id),
144	    FOREIGN KEY (order_id)        REFERENCES orders(id),
145	    UNIQUE KEY uk_conversation (participant_one, participant_two, order_id)
146	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
147	
148	CREATE INDEX idx_conv_p1 ON conversations(participant_one);
149	CREATE INDEX idx_conv_p2 ON conversations(participant_two);
150	
151	CREATE TABLE messages (
152	    id                BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
153	    conversation_id   BIGINT       NOT NULL,
154	    sender_id         BIGINT       NOT NULL,
155	    content           TEXT         NOT NULL,
156	    read_at           TIMESTAMP    NULL,
157	    created_at        TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
158	    FOREIGN KEY (conversation_id) REFERENCES conversations(id),
159	    FOREIGN KEY (sender_id)       REFERENCES users(id)
160	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
161	
162	CREATE INDEX idx_messages_conv ON messages(conversation_id);
163	CREATE INDEX idx_messages_unread ON messages(conversation_id, read_at);
164	
165	-- =====================================================
166	-- NOTIFICATION QUEUE: local queued to-send records
167	-- =====================================================
168	CREATE TABLE notification_queue (
169	    id              BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
170	    user_id         BIGINT        NOT NULL,
171	    channel         VARCHAR(10)   NOT NULL COMMENT 'EMAIL or SMS',
172	    recipient       VARCHAR(255)  NOT NULL COMMENT 'email address or phone number (encrypted)',
173	    subject         VARCHAR(500),
174	    body            TEXT          NOT NULL,
175	    status          VARCHAR(20)   NOT NULL DEFAULT 'QUEUED' COMMENT 'QUEUED, SENT, FAILED',
176	    retry_count     INT           DEFAULT 0,
177	    reference_type  VARCHAR(50),
178	    reference_id    BIGINT,
179	    created_at      TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
180	    processed_at    TIMESTAMP     NULL
181	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
182	
183	CREATE INDEX idx_notif_user ON notification_queue(user_id);
184	CREATE INDEX idx_notif_status ON notification_queue(status);
185	
186	-- =====================================================
187	-- BLACKLIST: admin governance with audit
188	-- =====================================================
189	CREATE TABLE blacklist (
190	    id              BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
191	    user_id         BIGINT       NOT NULL,
192	    reason          TEXT         NOT NULL,
193	    blacklisted_by  BIGINT       NOT NULL,
194	    duration_days   INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 7,
195	    starts_at       TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
196	    expires_at      TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL,
197	    active          BOOLEAN      DEFAULT TRUE,
198	    lifted_by       BIGINT,
199	    lifted_at       TIMESTAMP    NULL,
200	    lift_reason     TEXT,
201	    created_at      TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
202	    FOREIGN KEY (user_id)        REFERENCES users(id),
203	    FOREIGN KEY (blacklisted_by) REFERENCES users(id)
204	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
205	
206	CREATE INDEX idx_blacklist_user ON blacklist(user_id);
207	CREATE INDEX idx_blacklist_active ON blacklist(active, expires_at);
208	
209	-- =====================================================
210	-- POINTS / AWARDS LEDGER
211	-- =====================================================
212	CREATE TABLE points_ledger (
213	    id              BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
214	    user_id         BIGINT        NOT NULL,
215	    points          INT           NOT NULL,
216	    balance_after   INT           NOT NULL,
217	    action          VARCHAR(100)  NOT NULL,
218	    reference_type  VARCHAR(50),
219	    reference_id    BIGINT,
220	    description     VARCHAR(500),
221	    created_at      TIMESTAMP     DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
222	    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
223	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
224	
225	CREATE INDEX idx_points_user ON points_ledger(user_id);
226	
227	-- Track current balance per user for quick reads
228	ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN points_balance INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;

2	<!DOCTYPE mapper PUBLIC "-//mybatis.org//DTD Mapper 3.0//EN"
3	        "http://mybatis.org/dtd/mybatis-3-mapper.dtd">
4	<mapper namespace="com.booking.mapper.PointsLedgerMapper">
5	
6	    <select id="findByUserId" resultType="PointsLedgerEntry">
7	        SELECT * FROM points_ledger WHERE user_id = #{userId} ORDER BY created_at DESC
8	    </select>
9	
10	    <select id="getBalance" resultType="int">
11	        SELECT COALESCE(points_balance, 0) FROM users WHERE id = #{userId}
12	    </select>
13	
14	    <insert id="insert" useGeneratedKeys="true" keyProperty="id">
15	        INSERT INTO points_ledger (user_id, points, balance_after, action, reference_type, reference_id, description)
16	        VALUES (#{userId}, #{points}, #{balanceAfter}, #{action}, #{referenceType}, #{referenceId}, #{description})
17	    </insert>
18	
19	    <update id="updateUserBalance">
20	        UPDATE users SET points_balance = #{balance} WHERE id = #{userId}
21	    </update>
22	
23	    <select id="getLeaderboard" resultType="java.util.Map">
24	        SELECT u.id AS userId, u.full_name AS fullName, u.username,
25	               COALESCE(u.points_balance, 0) AS points,
26	               COUNT(CASE WHEN pl.action = 'ORDER_COMPLETED' THEN 1 END) AS completionCount,
27	               MIN(CASE WHEN pl.action = 'ORDER_COMPLETED' THEN pl.created_at END) AS earliestCompletion
28	        FROM users u
29	        LEFT JOIN points_ledger pl ON u.id = pl.user_id
30	        GROUP BY u.id, u.full_name, u.username, u.points_balance
31	        ORDER BY points DESC, completionCount DESC, earliestCompletion ASC
32	        LIMIT 50
33	    </select>
34	</mapper>

1	-- Migration V6: Server-side search terms store and address uniqueness constraint
2	USE booking_portal;
3	
4	-- =====================================================
5	-- SEARCH TERMS: global popular terms ranked by frequency/recency
6	-- =====================================================
7	CREATE TABLE search_terms (
8	    id            BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
9	    term          VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
10	    frequency     INT          NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
11	    last_used_at  TIMESTAMP    NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
12	    created_at    TIMESTAMP    DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
13	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;
14	
15	CREATE INDEX idx_search_terms_popular ON search_terms(frequency DESC, last_used_at DESC);
16	
17	-- =====================================================
18	-- ADDRESS DEFAULT UNIQUENESS: only one default per user
19	-- =====================================================
20	-- Strategy: use a BEFORE INSERT and BEFORE UPDATE trigger to enforce
21	-- at most one is_default=true per user_id. MySQL unique indexes treat
22	-- NULL as distinct, so a generated-column approach cannot reliably
23	-- prevent multiple defaults. Triggers provide a deterministic guarantee.
24	
25	DELIMITER //
26	
27	CREATE TRIGGER trg_address_default_insert
28	BEFORE INSERT ON addresses
29	FOR EACH ROW
30	BEGIN
31	    IF NEW.is_default = TRUE THEN
32	        IF (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM addresses
33	            WHERE user_id = NEW.user_id AND is_default = TRUE) > 0 THEN
34	            SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000'
35	            SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Another default address already exists for this user';
36	        END IF;
37	    END IF;
38	END//
39	
40	CREATE TRIGGER trg_address_default_update
41	BEFORE UPDATE ON addresses
42	FOR EACH ROW
43	BEGIN
44	    IF NEW.is_default = TRUE AND (OLD.is_default = FALSE OR OLD.is_default IS NULL) THEN
45	        IF (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM addresses
46	            WHERE user_id = NEW.user_id AND is_default = TRUE AND id != NEW.id) > 0 THEN
47	            SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000'
48	            SET MESSAGE_TEXT = 'Another default address already exists for this user';
49	        END IF;
50	    END IF;
51	END//
52	
53	DELIMITER ;
54	
55	INSERT INTO schema_version (version, description)
56	VALUES (6, 'Search terms store, address default uniqueness triggers');

17	        <result property="pointsBalance" column="points_balance"/>
18	        <result property="roleName" column="role_name"/>

20	
21	    <sql id="selectUser">
22	        SELECT u.*, r.name AS role_name
23	        FROM users u
24	        JOIN roles r ON u.role_id = r.id
25	    </sql>
26	
27	    <select id="findById" resultMap="userResult">
28	        <include refid="selectUser"/>
29	        WHERE u.id = #{id}
30	    </select>
31	
32	    <select id="findByUsername" resultMap="userResult">
33	        <include refid="selectUser"/>
34	        WHERE u.username = #{username}
35	    </select>
36	
37	    <select id="findByEmail" resultMap="userResult">

39	        WHERE u.email = #{email}
40	    </select>
41	
42	    <select id="findAll" resultMap="userResult">
43	        <include refid="selectUser"/>
44	        ORDER BY u.created_at DESC
45	    </select>
46

50	        ORDER BY u.full_name
51	    </select>

53	    <insert id="insert" useGeneratedKeys="true" keyProperty="id">

The better solution: in the CSRF filter, allow requests that have neither Origin nor Referer when the request comes from a non-browser source. The BaseApiIT class at `/home/mint/Desktop/projects/task-11/repo/api_tests/java/com/booking/api/BaseApiIT.java` now has:
- `protected static final String TEST_ORIGIN = "http://localhost";`
- `protected static MockHttpServletRequestBuilder withOrigin(MockHttpServletRequestBuilder builder)` which adds the Origin header

I need you to add `.header("Origin", TEST_ORIGIN)` to ALL POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE MockMvc requests across ALL integration test files in `/home/mint/Desktop/projects/task-11/repo/api_tests/java/com/booking/api/`. 

IMPORTANT rules:
1. Do NOT add Origin headers to requests targeting `/api/auth/login`, `/api/auth/register`, or `/api/auth/logout` since the CSRF filter skips `/api/auth/*`

The grep is showing lines that appear to not have Origin, but they may be on the next line. Let me do a more precise check to find any POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/multipart calls that are NOT followed (within a few lines) by `Origin`:

content.name: Bash
content.arguments:
{"command": "cd /home/mint/Desktop/projects/task-11/repo/api_tests/java/com/booking/api && python3 -c "
import re
import os

files = [f for f in os.listdir('.') if f.endswith('.java') and f != 'BaseApiIT.java']
auth_pattern = re.compile(r'/api/auth/')
method_pattern = re.compile(r'(post|put|patch|delete|multipart)\s*\(')

for fname in sorted(files):
    with open(fname) as f:
        content = f.read()
    
    # Find all mvc.perform blocks
    lines = content.split('\n')
    i = 0
    while i < len(lines):
        line = lines[i]
        if 'mvc.perform(' in line and method_pattern.search(line):
            # Check if this is an auth endpoint
            if auth_pattern.search(line):
                i += 1
                continue
            # Check if Origin header appears in the next few lines (within the builder chain)
            block = line
            j = i + 1
            while j < len(lines) and ')' not in lines[j-1].rstrip().rstrip(';'):
                block += '\n' + lines[j]
                j += 1
            # Grab a bit more
            end = min(i + 15, len(lines))
            block = '\n'.join(lines[i:end])
            if 'Origin' not in block:
                print(f'{fname}:{i+1}: MISSING Origin: {line.strip()[:80]}')
        i += 1
"
"}

The `loginAs` in BaseApiIT uses POST to `/api/auth/login` — since we changed the login method to take `HttpServletRequest` instead of `HttpSession`, I need to verify the session handling works with MockMvc. Let me re-read the modified AuthController login method:

There's a problem with the `loginAs` helper in BaseApiIT. After session rotation, the `MockHttpSession` passed in will be invalidated, and the newly created session from `request.getSession(true)` will be a different object. The `loginAs` method needs to return the new session, not the old one. Let me update it:

content.text: The file /home/mint/Desktop/projects/task-11/repo/api_tests/java/com/booking/api/BaseApiIT.java has been updated successfully.
Now let me also check the `loginRotatesSession` test in FoundationFixesApiIT - it verifies `session1.isInvalid()` which should work correctly:

Good. Now let me also check the `FoundationFixesApiIT` tests for the CSRF rejection test - it deliberately omits the Origin header so it should be rejected. But the `csrfRejectsMissingOriginOnStateChangingEndpoint` test hits `/api/points/adjust` which requires authentication. Let me make sure it logs in first... It does - `loginAs("admin")` is called.

Now let me verify the existing tests that reference legacy bookings/services/attachments still make sense. Let me check `BookingServiceAttachmentApiIT`:

Good - those tests still verify the endpoints return 404, which is correct since the controllers are removed. The database tables no longer exist either, but that doesn't affect the 404 response since there are no controllers for those paths.

Let me now also check the `idempotency404ReplayReturnsCachedResponse` test more carefully. When the CSRF filter runs first, and the request passes CSRF, then the AuthFilter runs. For a non-existent order, the controller's `executeAction` calls `checkToken` with the orderId, then checks the order exists. If order is null, it records with `orderId` (our fix). On replay, `checkToken` uses the same scoped key and should find the cached 404. But wait - the 404 path returns `ResponseEntity.notFound().build()` which has no body, but the cached response has a body "Order not found". On replay, the cached status is 404 and body is "Order not found". Let me check how the cached response is returned:

Looking at the `executeAction` method in OrderController line 172-173:
```java
if (idem.isDuplicate) {
    return ResponseEntity.status(idem.cachedStatus).body(idem.cachedBody);
}
```

So on replay, it returns status 404 with body "Order not found" as a string. That should work.

But wait - in the test, we check `.andExpect(status().isNotFound())` on the replay. The first request also returns `ResponseEntity.notFound().build()` which is a 404. The replay returns `ResponseEntity.status(404).body("Order not found")`. Both should pass `status().isNotFound()`. 

But there's another issue: the `cust1` user tries to confirm an order. The `executeAction` method first checks the order exists (returns null for 999999). For a non-existent order, the error handling path runs before the access check. The `confirm` action requires PHOTOGRAPHER role, but since the order is null, the 404 is returned before the role check. On the first call, `checkToken("replay-404-xxx", "CONFIRM", 999999L)` is called with the scoped key. The token is inserted. Then order is not found, so `recordResponse("replay-404-xxx", "CONFIRM", 999999L, 404, "Order not found")` is called with the correct orderId.

On replay, `checkToken("replay-404-xxx", "CONFIRM", 999999L)` finds the existing token with status 404 and returns it. The controller returns `ResponseEntity.status(404).body("Order not found")`. That's correct.

Now let me verify that the `loginRotatesSession` test and `loginCreatesNewAuthenticatedSession` test will work correctly with MockMvc. In MockMvc, when you call `request.getSession(false)` after `oldSession.invalidate()`, MockMvc might return the invalidated session. Let me check - actually MockMvc wraps the request. The `oldSession.invalidate()` call on `MockHttpSession` marks it as invalid. Then `request.getSession(true)` should create a new session.

Actually, there's a subtlety with MockMvc. When a `MockHttpSession` is provided via `.session(session)`, the `MockHttpServletRequest` will return that session from `getSession(false)`. After we invalidate it, `getSession(true)` should create a new `MockHttpSession`. Let me check if the test's `result.getRequest().getSession(false)` returns the new session. In MockMvc's implementation, once the old session is invalidated and a new one is created via `getSession(true)`, subsequent `getSession(false)` calls should return the new one. This should work.

Good - all imports look correct. Let me now check the `idempotency404DoesNotCollideDifferentOrderIds` test - it uses the same idem key for two different order IDs. The scoped key is `idemKey::CONFIRM::999998` vs `idemKey::CONFIRM::999997`, so they should not collide:

All changes are in place. Here's a summary of everything implemented:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. CSRF Protection (new `CsrfFilter.java`)
- Validates `Origin` or `Referer` header on all POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE requests
- Rejects requests with missing or mismatched origin as 403
- Skips `/api/auth/*` endpoints
- Registered in `WebConfig` at order 0 (before AuthFilter)

### 2. Session Fixation Protection (`AuthController.java`)
- Login now invalidates the old session and creates a fresh one before storing user
- `BaseApiIT.loginAs()` updated to return the new session from `request.getSession(false)` after rotation

### 3. Legacy V1 Schema Purge
- Removed `services`, `bookings`, `attachments` tables from:
  - `01-schema.sql` (production migration)
  - `02-seed.sql` (seed data)
  - Both `schema-test.sql` (unit & API test schemas)
  - Both `data-test.sql` (test seed data)
- Updated role descriptions from "bookings" to "orders"

### 4. Atomic Points Balance Updates (`PointsService.java`, `PointsLedgerMapper`)
- `awardPoints()` now uses `UPDATE users SET points_balance = points_balance + ?` (atomic)
- `deductPoints()` now uses `UPDATE users SET points_balance = GREATEST(0, points_balance + ?)` (atomic floor)
- Eliminates read-then-write race conditions under concurrent requests
- Unit tests updated to verify atomic methods are called

### 5. Idempotency 404 Cache Key Fix (`OrderController.java:180`)
- Changed `recordResponse(..., null, 404, ...)` to `recordResponse(..., orderId, 404, ...)` 
- Now matches the scoped key used by `checkToken`, enabling correct 404 replay

### 6. Expanded Role Model
- Added `SERVICE_PROVIDER` role (id=4) to seed data and test data
- Created `ProviderDto.java` with role field
- Added `/api/users/providers` endpoint returning all PHOTOGRAPHER + SERVICE_PROVIDER users
- Updated `OrderService.enforceAccess()`, `getForUser()`, and all order lifecycle methods to accept SERVICE_PROVIDER

### 7. Delivery/Pickup ETA Fields
- Added `delivery_eta` and `pickup_eta` columns to orders (new migration `09-migration-v7.sql`)
- Added fields to `Order.java`, `OrderMapper.xml` (resultMap, insert, update)
- `OrderService.createOrder()` auto-populates ETAs based on delivery mode (COURIER: +2h, PICKUP: +30m)
- Test schemas updated with new columns

### 8. Tests (`FoundationFixesApiIT.java`, `CsrfFilterTest.java`)
- **CSRF**: reject missing Origin, reject foreign Origin, allow matching Origin, allow Referer fallback, skip auth endpoints, allow GET
- **Session rotation**: verify old session invalidated, verify new session authenticated
- **Concurrent points**: 5 threads concurrently award points, verify final balance is exactly correct
- **SSE reconnection**: verify stream connects, reconnects with same session, requires auth
- **404 idempotency**: verify cached 404 replays correctly, verify different orderIds don't collide
- **Provider/ETA**: verify providers endpoint, verify COURIER has deliveryEta, PICKUP has pickupEta, ONSITE has neither
- All existing tests updated with `Origin` headers to pass CSRF filter

3	# run_tests.sh — Automated quality gate for the Booking Portal

6	# enforces ≥90% line coverage for both suites, and prints a summary.
7	#
8	# Usage:

10	#   ./run_tests.sh unit         # unit tests only
11	#   ./run_tests.sh api          # api tests only (includes unit)
12	#
13	# Requirements: Docker (no host-installed Java/Maven needed)
14	#
15	set -euo pipefail
16	
17	COVERAGE_THRESHOLD=90
18	IMAGE_NAME="booking-portal-tests"
19	CONTAINER_NAME="booking-tests-$$"
20	RED='\033[0;31m'; GREEN='\033[0;32m'; YELLOW='\033[1;33m'; NC='\033[0m'; BOLD='\033[1m'
21	
22	cd "$(dirname "$0")"
23	
24	# ─── Determine which suites to run ────────────────────────────
25	MODE="${1:-all}"
26	case "$MODE" in
27	  unit) MVN_GOAL="test"   ;;
28	  api)  MVN_GOAL="verify" ;;
29	  all)  MVN_GOAL="verify" ;;
30	  *)    echo "Usage: $0 [unit|api|all]"; exit 1 ;;
31	esac
32	
33	echo -e "${BOLD}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
34	echo -e "${BOLD}  Booking Portal — Quality Gate (mode: ${MODE})${NC}"
35	echo -e "${BOLD}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
36	echo ""
37	
38	# ─── Build test image ─────────────────────────────────────────
39	echo -e "${YELLOW}[1/4] Building test container...${NC}"
40	docker build -f Dockerfile.test -t "$IMAGE_NAME" . --quiet
41	
42	# ─── Run tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────
43	echo -e "${YELLOW}[2/4] Running ${MODE} tests inside container...${NC}"
44	echo ""
45	
46	PASS=true
47	docker run --rm --name "$CONTAINER_NAME" \
48	  -v "$(pwd)/test-results:/build/target/site" \
49	  "$IMAGE_NAME" \
50	  "$MVN_GOAL" -B 2>&1 | tee /tmp/booking-test-output.log || PASS=false
51	
52	echo ""
53	
54	# ─── Parse results ────────────────────────────────────────────
55	echo -e "${YELLOW}[3/4] Parsing test results...${NC}"
56	echo ""
57	
58	# Extract totals from summary lines (lines without " -- in " are totals)
59	# Surefire prints its total first, Failsafe prints its total second
60	UNIT_SUMMARY=$(grep 'Tests run:' /tmp/booking-test-output.log | grep -v ' -- in ' | head -1 || echo "Tests run: 0, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0")
61	UNIT_RUN=$(echo "$UNIT_SUMMARY" | grep -oP 'Tests run: \K[0-9]+')
62	UNIT_FAIL=$(echo "$UNIT_SUMMARY" | grep -oP 'Failures: \K[0-9]+')
63	UNIT_ERR=$(echo "$UNIT_SUMMARY" | grep -oP 'Errors: \K[0-9]+')
64	UNIT_SKIP=$(echo "$UNIT_SUMMARY" | grep -oP 'Skipped: \K[0-9]+')
65	
66	if [[ "$MODE" != "unit" ]]; then
67	  API_SUMMARY=$(grep 'Tests run:' /tmp/booking-test-output.log | grep -v ' -- in ' | tail -1 || echo "Tests run: 0, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0")
68	  API_RUN=$(echo "$API_SUMMARY" | grep -oP 'Tests run: \K[0-9]+')
69	  API_FAIL=$(echo "$API_SUMMARY" | grep -oP 'Failures: \K[0-9]+')
70	  API_ERR=$(echo "$API_SUMMARY" | grep -oP 'Errors: \K[0-9]+')
71	  API_SKIP=$(echo "$API_SUMMARY" | grep -oP 'Skipped: \K[0-9]+')
72	else
73	  API_RUN=0; API_FAIL=0; API_ERR=0; API_SKIP=0
74	fi
75	
76	# ─── Coverage extraction ─────────────────────────────────────
77	# JaCoCo check output contains coverage data - parse from log
78	UNIT_COV=$(grep -A2 'check-unit' /tmp/booking-test-output.log | grep -oP 'covered ratio is ([0-9.]+)' | grep -oP '[0-9.]+' | head -1 || echo "")
79	API_COV=$(grep -A2 'check-api' /tmp/booking-test-output.log | grep -oP 'covered ratio is ([0-9.]+)' | grep -oP '[0-9.]+' | head -1 || echo "")
80	
81	# If coverage not found from check output, try to get from report
82	if [[ -z "$UNIT_COV" ]]; then
83	  UNIT_COV=$(grep -oP 'Rule violated.*?is ([0-9.]+)' /tmp/booking-test-output.log | head -1 | grep -oP '[0-9.]+$' || echo "")
84	fi
85	if [[ -z "$API_COV" ]]; then
86	  API_COV=$(grep -oP 'Rule violated.*?is ([0-9.]+)' /tmp/booking-test-output.log | tail -1 | grep -oP '[0-9.]+$' || echo "")
87	fi
88	
89	# ─── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
90	echo -e "${BOLD}[4/4] Quality Gate Summary${NC}"
91	echo -e "${BOLD}══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════${NC}"
92	echo ""
93	echo -e "  ${BOLD}Unit Tests (Surefire):${NC}"
94	echo -e "    Run: ${UNIT_RUN}  Failures: ${UNIT_FAIL}  Errors: ${UNIT_ERR}  Skipped: ${UNIT_SKIP}"
95	if [[ -n "$UNIT_COV" ]]; then
96	  UNIT_PCT=$(echo "$UNIT_COV" | awk '{printf "%.1f", $1 * 100}')
97	  echo -e "    Line Coverage: ${UNIT_PCT}%  (threshold: ${COVERAGE_THRESHOLD}%)"
98	fi
99	echo ""
100	
101	if [[ "$MODE" != "unit" ]]; then
102	  echo -e "  ${BOLD}API Tests (Failsafe):${NC}"
103	  echo -e "    Run: ${API_RUN}  Failures: ${API_FAIL}  Errors: ${API_ERR}  Skipped: ${API_SKIP}"
104	  if [[ -n "$API_COV" ]]; then
105	    API_PCT=$(echo "$API_COV" | awk '{printf "%.1f", $1 * 100}')
106	    echo -e "    Line Coverage: ${API_PCT}%  (threshold: ${COVERAGE_THRESHOLD}%)"
107	  fi
108	  echo ""
109	fi
110	
111	echo -e "  ${BOLD}Coverage Reports:${NC}"
112	echo -e "    Unit: test-results/jacoco-unit/index.html"
113	echo -e "    API:  test-results/jacoco-api/index.html"
114	echo ""
115	
116	# ─── Verdict ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
117	TOTAL_FAIL=$((UNIT_FAIL + UNIT_ERR + API_FAIL + API_ERR))
118	
119	if [[ "$PASS" == "true" && "$TOTAL_FAIL" -eq 0 ]]; then
120	  echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}  ✓ QUALITY GATE PASSED${NC}"
121	  echo -e "${GREEN}    All tests passed. Coverage thresholds met (≥${COVERAGE_THRESHOLD}%).${NC}"
122	  echo ""
123	  exit 0
124	else
125	  echo -e "${RED}${BOLD}  ✗ QUALITY GATE FAILED${NC}"
126	  if [[ "$TOTAL_FAIL" -gt 0 ]]; then
127	    echo -e "${RED}    ${TOTAL_FAIL} test(s) failed or errored.${NC}"
128	  fi
129	  if [[ "$PASS" != "true" ]]; then
130	    echo -e "${RED}    Maven build failed (test failures or coverage below ${COVERAGE_THRESHOLD}%).${NC}"
131	    echo -e "${RED}    Check /tmp/booking-test-output.log for details.${NC}"
132	  fi
133	  echo ""
134	  exit 1
135	fi

The build fails because the pom.xml change caused a cache bust at the `COPY pom.xml` layer (pom.xml didn't change, but the `--no-cache` from the first attempt might have invalidated layers). The real issue is that `apache/maven/plugins/maven-metadata.xml` times out, then it resolves the wrong `dependency-maven-plugin` instead of `maven-dependency-plugin`.

The problem is actually that the pom.xml hasn't changed, but we changed from `eclipse-temurin-17` to `eclipse-temurin-21` which invalidates all cached layers. The previous successful build used `eclipse-temurin-17`. Let me revert the Dockerfile.test back to temurin-17 and use the cached image:

Wait - looking at the docker images output again: `booking-portal-tests` exists locally and was built with temurin-17 originally. But `maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-17` is NOT in the local images. Only `maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-21` is. So the previous `booking-portal-tests` build must have pulled temurin-17 from Docker Hub when it was available.

Since the network is unreliable, the best approach is to use the existing cached `booking-portal-tests` image. But we changed source files, so we need to rebuild the source layers. Let me use a multi-stage approach — skip the dependency download by using the existing image as a cache source:

### 3. Frontend (`app.js`)
- **Navigation**: `SERVICE_PROVIDER` gets the same dashboard/nav as `PHOTOGRAPHER`.

- **Order detail**: Shows actual `deliveryEta` and `pickupEta` timestamps from the API response instead of computed text.

### 4. Docker/Infrastructure
- **`docker-compose.yml`**: Mounted `09-migration-v7.sql`.
- **`Dockerfile.test`**: Updated to reuse cached Maven repo for faster builds.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3	# init-secrets.sh — Single source of truth for runtime credentials.
4	#
5	# Generates all secrets once and writes them to a shared volume so
6	# that both the MySQL and webapp containers read identical values.
7	# Runs as a short-lived init container during `docker compose up`.
8	#
9	# Output: /run/secrets/booking/secrets.env  (sourced by both services)
10	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
11	set -euo pipefail
12	
13	SECRETS_DIR="/run/secrets/booking"
14	SECRETS_FILE="${SECRETS_DIR}/secrets.env"
15	
16	# If secrets already exist (restart without volume wipe), keep them.
17	if [ -f "$SECRETS_FILE" ]; then
18	  echo "[init-secrets] Secrets file already exists — skipping generation."
19	  exit 0
20	fi
21	
22	mkdir -p "$SECRETS_DIR"
23	
24	# Generate a secure random hex string of N bytes (2*N hex chars).
25	gen_hex() {
26	  head -c "$1" /dev/urandom | od -An -tx1 | tr -d ' \n'
27	}
28	
29	MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD="$(gen_hex 24)"
30	MYSQL_USER="booking_app"
31	MYSQL_PASSWORD="$(gen_hex 24)"
32	ENCRYPTION_KEY="$(gen_hex 32)"   # 64 hex chars — exceeds 32-char minimum
33	
34	cat > "$SECRETS_FILE" <<EOF
35	MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD}
36	MYSQL_USER=${MYSQL_USER}
37	MYSQL_PASSWORD=${MYSQL_PASSWORD}
38	ENCRYPTION_KEY=${ENCRYPTION_KEY}
39	EOF
40	
41	chmod 600 "$SECRETS_FILE"
42	echo "[init-secrets] Secrets written to ${SECRETS_FILE}"
43

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3	# mysql-entrypoint.sh — Loads shared secrets then delegates to the
4	# official MySQL entrypoint.  Refuses to start if the secrets file
5	# is missing, ensuring the database never boots with empty or
6	# default credentials.
7	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8	set -euo pipefail
9	
10	SECRETS_FILE="/run/secrets/booking/secrets.env"
11	
12	if [ ! -f "$SECRETS_FILE" ]; then
13	  echo "[mysql-entrypoint] FATAL: ${SECRETS_FILE} not found."
14	  echo "[mysql-entrypoint] The init-secrets service must run first."
15	  exit 1
16	fi
17	
18	# Export every KEY=VALUE line into this shell's environment.
19	set -a
20	# shellcheck source=/dev/null
21	. "$SECRETS_FILE"
22	set +a
23	
24	echo "[mysql-entrypoint] Secrets loaded — starting MySQL."
25	
26	# Delegate to the official MySQL Docker entrypoint.
27	exec docker-entrypoint.sh "$@"
28

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3	# entrypoint.sh (webapp) — Loads shared secrets from the init
4	# container's volume, then launches the Spring Boot application.
5	# Refuses to start if the secrets file is missing or if the
6	# ENCRYPTION_KEY does not meet the 32-character minimum.
7	# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8	set -euo pipefail
9	
10	SECRETS_FILE="/run/secrets/booking/secrets.env"
11	
12	if [ ! -f "$SECRETS_FILE" ]; then
13	  echo "[webapp-entrypoint] FATAL: ${SECRETS_FILE} not found."
14	  echo "[webapp-entrypoint] The init-secrets service must run first."
15	  exit 1
16	fi
17	
18	# Export every KEY=VALUE line into this shell's environment.
19	set -a
20	# shellcheck source=/dev/null
21	. "$SECRETS_FILE"
22	set +a
23	
24	# ── Pre-flight: validate secret strength before JVM startup ──────
25	if [ "${#ENCRYPTION_KEY}" -lt 32 ]; then
26	  echo "[webapp-entrypoint] FATAL: ENCRYPTION_KEY is ${#ENCRYPTION_KEY} chars (minimum 32)."
27	  exit 1
28	fi
29	
30	if [ -z "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then
31	  echo "[webapp-entrypoint] FATAL: MYSQL_PASSWORD is empty."
32	  exit 1
33	fi
34	
35	# Map shared var names to Spring-expected env vars.
36	export SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME="${MYSQL_USER}"
37	export SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD="${MYSQL_PASSWORD}"
38	
39	echo "[webapp-entrypoint] Secrets loaded — starting application."
40	exec "$@"
41

1	FROM maven:3.9-eclipse-temurin-21 AS build
2	WORKDIR /build
3	COPY pom.xml .
4	RUN mvn dependency:go-offline -B
5	COPY src ./src
6	RUN mvn package -DskipTests -B
7	
8	FROM eclipse-temurin:17-jre
9	WORKDIR /app
10	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl openssl && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
11	RUN mkdir -p /app/uploads
12	COPY --from=build /build/target/*.jar app.jar
13	COPY docker/entrypoint.sh /app/entrypoint.sh
14	RUN chmod +x /app/entrypoint.sh
15	ENTRYPOINT ["/app/entrypoint.sh"]
16	EXPOSE 8080
17	CMD ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]
18

Now I understand the root cause. The `secrets` volume is declared as `tmpfs`, which means each container gets its own **in-memory mount** of that volume. Data written by `init-secrets` is never visible to `mysql` or `webapp` because tmpfs volumes with the `local` driver don't actually share data across container boundaries the way regular Docker volumes do — each container mounting a tmpfs volume gets a fresh, empty tmpfs filesystem.

The fix is to switch from a tmpfs volume to a regular named volume (which uses disk-backed storage and is genuinely shared), and add a wait-for-file retry loop in both consumer entrypoints to handle any propagation delay.

content.text: The file /home/mint/Desktop/projects/task-11/repo/docker-compose.yml has been updated successfully.
Now update the MySQL entrypoint with a wait-for-file retry loop:

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/mint/Desktop/projects/task-11/repo/docker/mysql-entrypoint.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# mysql-entrypoint.sh — Loads shared secrets then delegates to the
# official MySQL entrypoint.  Waits up to 30 seconds for the
# secrets file to appear (handling volume propagation delay), then
# refuses to start if it is still missing.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

SECRETS_FILE="/run/secrets/booking/secrets.env"
MAX_WAIT=30
WAIT_INTERVAL=1

# ── Wait for secrets file with retry loop ────────────────────────
elapsed=0
while [ ! -f "$SECRETS_FILE" ]; do
  if [ "$elapsed" -ge "$MAX_WAIT" ]; then
    echo "[mysql-entrypoint] FATAL: ${SECRETS_FILE} not found after ${MAX_WAIT}s."
    echo "[mysql-entrypoint] The init-secrets service must run first."
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "[mysql-entrypoint] Waiting for ${SECRETS_FILE}... (${elapsed}/${MAX_WAIT}s)"
  sleep "$WAIT_INTERVAL"
  elapsed=$((elapsed + WAIT_INTERVAL))
done

echo "[mysql-entrypoint] Secrets file found after ${elapsed}s."

# Export every KEY=VALUE line into this shell's environment.
set -a
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
. "$SECRETS_FILE"
set +a

echo "[mysql-entrypoint] Secrets loaded — starting MySQL."

# Delegate to the official MySQL Docker entrypoint.
exec docker-entrypoint.sh "$@"
"}

{"file_path": "/home/mint/Desktop/projects/task-11/repo/docker/entrypoint.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# entrypoint.sh (webapp) — Loads shared secrets from the init
# container's volume, then launches the Spring Boot application.
# Waits up to 30 seconds for the secrets file to appear (handling
# volume propagation delay), then refuses to start if the file is
# still missing or if the ENCRYPTION_KEY does not meet the
# 32-character minimum.
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

SECRETS_FILE="/run/secrets/booking/secrets.env"
MAX_WAIT=30
WAIT_INTERVAL=1

# ── Wait for secrets file with retry loop ────────────────────────
elapsed=0
while [ ! -f "$SECRETS_FILE" ]; do
  if [ "$elapsed" -ge "$MAX_WAIT" ]; then
    echo "[webapp-entrypoint] FATAL: ${SECRETS_FILE} not found after ${MAX_WAIT}s."
    echo "[webapp-entrypoint] The init-secrets service must run first."
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "[webapp-entrypoint] Waiting for ${SECRETS_FILE}... (${elapsed}/${MAX_WAIT}s)"
  sleep "$WAIT_INTERVAL"
  elapsed=$((elapsed + WAIT_INTERVAL))
done

echo "[webapp-entrypoint] Secrets file found after ${elapsed}s."

# Export every KEY=VALUE line into this shell's environment.
set -a
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
. "$SECRETS_FILE"
set +a

# ── Pre-flight: validate secret strength before JVM startup ──────
if [ "${#ENCRYPTION_KEY}" -lt 32 ]; then
  echo "[webapp-entrypoint] FATAL: ENCRYPTION_KEY is ${#ENCRYPTION_KEY} chars (minimum 32)."
  exit 1
fi

if [ -z "${MYSQL_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then
  echo "[webapp-entrypoint] FATAL: MYSQL_PASSWORD is empty."
  exit 1
fi

# Map shared var names to Spring-expected env vars.
export SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME="${MYSQL_USER}"
export SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD="${MYSQL_PASSWORD}"

echo "[webapp-entrypoint] Secrets loaded — starting application."
exec "$@"
"}

# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# init-secrets.sh — Single source of truth for runtime credentials.
#
# Generates all secrets once and writes them to a shared volume so
# that both the MySQL and webapp containers read identical values.
# Runs as a short-lived init container during `docker compose up`.
#
# Output: /run/secrets/booking/secrets.env  (sourced by both services)
# ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
set -euo pipefail

if [ -f "$SECRETS_FILE" ]; then
  echo "[init-secrets] Secrets file already exists — skipping generation."
  exit 0
fi

mkdir -p "$SECRETS_DIR"

# Generate a secure random hex string of N bytes (2*N hex chars).
gen_hex() {
  head -c "$1" /dev/urandom | od -An -tx1 | tr -d ' \n'
}

MYSQL_USER="booking_app"
MYSQL_PASSWORD="$(gen_hex 24)"
ENCRYPTION_KEY="$(gen_hex 32)"   # 64 hex chars — exceeds 32-char minimum

cat > "$SECRETS_FILE" <<EOF
MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=${MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD}
MYSQL_USER=${MYSQL_USER}
MYSQL_PASSWORD=${MYSQL_PASSWORD}
ENCRYPTION_KEY=${ENCRYPTION_KEY}
EOF

chmod 600 "$SECRETS_FILE"
